game_info.title: Type:Rider - Typography Platformer
game_info.concept: **Type:Rider:** Players control two rolling dots (••) through 2D platforming levels themed around the history of typography, using left/right swipes to move and a tap anywhere on the screen to jump, with a second tap mid-air for a double jump. The core loop involves navigating intricate environments constructed from letters and symbols, solving physics-based puzzles by pushing blocks, activating switches by landing on specific character parts, or timing jumps across moving typographic elements. Progression occurs across 10 distinct worlds, each representing a different font or era, unlocking sequentially upon completion, with difficulty scaling as platforming challenges become more precise and puzzles require multi-step solutions. The goal is to reach the end of each level, with failure (falling into gaps or hitting hazards) resulting in an immediate respawn at the last checkpoint.
game_info.description: Navigate two connected rolling dots through intricate typographic worlds! Each level is constructed from letters, symbols, and fonts representing different eras of typography. Reach the end of each level while avoiding hazards and solving physics-based puzzles. Collect asterisks (*) for points and hit checkpoints to save your progress. Fall into gaps or touch spikes, and you'll respawn at your last checkpoint. Complete all levels to master the history of typography!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/A: Move left
Arrow Right/D: Move right
Space/W: Jump (press again in mid-air for double jump)
Enter: Start game from START screen
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart from GAME_OVER screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement mechanics and physics. This test verifies that the two connected dots can move left and right properly, jump with realistic physics, and that the Matter.js physics engine is working correctly. The test will perform basic movement commands to ensure responsive controls.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Start the game and perform a sequence of movements: move right for 2 seconds, jump twice to test double jump, move left for 2 seconds, and jump again. This tests horizontal movement in both directions, single jump, and double jump mechanics. The test runs for approximately 10 seconds.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should move smoothly in response to inputs, jumps should have appropriate height and gravity, double jump should work mid-air, and the two dots should remain connected throughout. The physics should feel natural with proper acceleration and deceleration. Test is successful if all movements execute without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing level completion and win condition. This test automates gameplay to reach the end of the first level, triggering the win state. It verifies that the game can detect when the player reaches the goal, properly transitions to the next level or win screen, and handles level progression correctly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Start the game and perform continuous right movement with periodic jumps to navigate the level terrain. The bot will jump at regular intervals to clear obstacles and platforms. Movement continues until the level end is reached or 30 seconds elapse. This simulates a player completing the level.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player should successfully navigate through the first level, reach the goal area at the end, and trigger the level complete state. The game should show completion feedback and either load the next level or show a win screen. Test succeeds when gamePhase changes to indicate level completion without crashes.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing checkpoint system and respawn mechanics. This test verifies that checkpoints save player progress and that respawning after falling into gaps or hitting hazards works correctly. The player should return to the last activated checkpoint with their position reset properly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Start the game, move right to activate a checkpoint, then deliberately fall into a gap by moving off a platform edge. After respawning, verify the player returns to the checkpoint location. Repeat this process multiple times to ensure consistent behavior.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: After falling, the player should respawn at the last checkpoint position rather than the level start. The respawn should be instant with proper physics state reset. Health or lives (if implemented) should decrease appropriately. Test succeeds if respawn location is correct and no physics glitches occur.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing collision detection with typographic platforms and hazards. This verifies that the player properly collides with letter-based platforms, can stand on them without falling through, and takes damage or dies when touching hazardous elements like spikes.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Navigate through the level by jumping onto various letter platforms. Test landing on different shaped letters (curves vs straight edges), walking along letter surfaces, and intentionally touching spike hazards to verify collision response. Perform jumps at different angles to test edge cases.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The player should land solidly on all platforms regardless of their typographic shape, with no falling through or getting stuck. Collision with hazards should trigger appropriate damage or death response. Test succeeds if all collisions are detected accurately and physics remain stable.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state management. This verifies that pressing ESC properly pauses the game, freezes all physics and animations, displays the pause overlay, and resumes correctly when unpaused. Also tests that R key restart works from game over state.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Start playing, move for a few seconds, then press ESC to pause. Wait 2 seconds in paused state, then press ESC again to resume. Continue playing and then deliberately fail (fall into gap). From game over screen, press R to restart and verify return to start screen.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: During pause, all movement and physics should freeze completely. The pause overlay should appear. On resume, gameplay should continue from exactly where it paused. From game over, pressing R should cleanly reset all game state and return to the start screen. Test succeeds if all transitions work without errors.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing collectible system and score tracking. This verifies that collecting asterisk (*) symbols properly increases the score, provides visual feedback, and that the score persists across checkpoints and respawns but resets appropriately when restarting the game.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Navigate through the level specifically targeting collectible asterisks. Collect several, note the score increase, then deliberately die and respawn at checkpoint. Verify score is maintained. Restart the entire game and verify score resets to zero.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each collected asterisk should disappear, increment the score, and possibly trigger a visual effect. Score should remain consistent through respawns but reset on full game restart. The UI should clearly display the current score. Test succeeds if score tracking is accurate and persistent.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing physics-based puzzle mechanics including pushable blocks and switches. This verifies that the player can push typographic blocks with realistic physics, that blocks can be used to reach higher platforms, and that landing on switch characters activates mechanisms.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Locate a pushable block element in the level and push it by moving into it repeatedly. Attempt to use the block as a stepping stone to reach a higher platform. Find and activate a switch by landing on it, observing any mechanism that gets triggered (moving platforms, opening paths, etc.).
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Blocks should move realistically when pushed, with appropriate friction and weight. The player should be able to jump onto pushed blocks to access new areas. Switches should activate with visual feedback when the player lands on them. Test succeeds if all puzzle mechanics work smoothly without physics glitches.
